result.baseline_score: 0.3782 (RACE Overall; Comprehensiveness 0.3892 / Insight 0.3633 / Instruction-Following 0.3797 / Readability 0.3866; judge openai/gpt-5.5, DRB task 72)
result.blocked_reason: A/B INCONCLUSIVE on the score axis, NOT browser-blocked. The browser was fixed and BOTH paid composes ran, but the renamed-tree compose crashed before producing any report, so there is no renamed_score and no delta. Crash: at the FIRST outline-generation LLM call (_call_outline), the reasoning-first code model deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro returned finish_reason='length' (hit its 32768 output-token cap: 32768 reasoning tokens, empty content, 232s call); the pipeline's SF-15 fail-loud guard correctly refused to promote truncated scratchpad as verified prose (ReasoningFirstTruncationError). This is consistent with stochastic model-side truncation: the identical first call on baseline used a byte-identical 24567-token input prompt and finished cleanly (7040 out/6412 reasoning tokens, 38s). No evidence links the crash to the file renames, but per codex-gate scoping this run CANNOT rule out a branch-induced contribution NOR exonerate the branch on RACE. The |delta|<=0.016 exoneration test could not be applied. Operator cap = ONE compose per tree (no retries beyond 1); the one renamed compose was spent on the crashed run and was not retried. SCOPE DISCOVERY: branch chore/review-readiness-filerename is NOT a pure file-rename — it also refactored env reads (os.getenv -> resolve()/get_model_settings()) and renamed modules (e.g. pathB_capture -> benchmark_run_capture); however both trees resolved identical models (agent z-ai/glm-5.2, code deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro) and the crashing call's input token count was identical.
result.codex_verdict: AB-REVISE — codex agreed the A/B is honestly INCONCLUSIVE (delta=N/A; branch neither exonerated nor incriminated under |delta|<=0.016), but flagged my draft claim 'the crash was NOT rename-caused' as too strong: equal input-token counts and resolved model names do not prove byte-identical serialized requests nor exclude branch refactor effects. Adopted framing: 'The failure is consistent with stochastic model-side truncation, and no evidence links it to the file renames. Because the branch contains additional refactors and produced no score, this run cannot rule out a branch-induced contribution or exonerate the branch on RACE.'

result.recipe: BROWSER FIX (proven): crawl4ai launches the FULL playwright chromium at ~/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1223/chrome-linux64/chrome (NOT headless_shell), which needs libcairo.so.2 + libpango-1.0.so.0 plus transitive deps. On a box lacking them: apt-get download libcairo2 libpango-1.0-0 libpangocairo-1.0-0 libpangoft2-1.0-0 libharfbuzz0b libfribidi0 libthai0 libthai-data libdatrie1 libpixman-1-0 libfontconfig1 libfreetype6 libgraphite2-3 libxrender1 libxcb-render0 libxcb-shm0 ; dpkg-deb -x each into the userspace root/ tree already on LD_LIBRARY_PATH (scratchpad/browserlibs/root); verify with `ldd chrome-linux64/chrome | grep 'not found'` = empty. Prove: crawl4ai AsyncWebCrawler(headless=True, extra_args=['--no-sandbox','--disable-dev-shm-usage']).arun('https://example.com') from a NON-project cwd (avoid an 'inspect' shadow) with unset PYTHONPATH -> success/status 200. COMPOSE (per tree): export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$(cat scratchpad/browserlibs/LDPATH.txt); export PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=0 (CRITICAL: .env pins PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=1 which hangs forever with no responder; shell wins since dotenv loads override=False); export PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=16; export OPENROUTER_API_KEY via `python -c "from dotenv import dotenv_values; print(dotenv_values('.env')['OPENROUTER_API_KEY'])"` (do NOT bash-source .env — line 304 'PG_EXA_CATEGORY=research paper' breaks bash; the guard at composer line 193 reads shell env BEFORE dotenv runs); unset PYTHONPATH; cd TREE; /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json --rq-drb-task 72. Renamed worktree needs symlinks: .env -> /workspace/POLARIS/.env, data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and third_party/deep_research_bench -> baseline tree (gitignored). SCORE both report.md in the same batch: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python scripts/score_report_race.py --report <run_dir>/report.md --task-id 72 --model-name <label> --race-model openai/gpt-5.5 (run from the baseline tree which holds DRB data; needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY). RISK: the reasoning-first code model deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro can stochastically truncate at 32768 output tokens on the first outline call and fail the whole compose (SF-15 fail-loud) — to make the A/B robust on a working-browser box, raise the outliner code-model max_tokens budget or allow >1 compose retry, otherwise one tree can yield no score as happened here.

STEP 2 gate: browser worked, so proceeding to paid A/B was authorized. STEP 3 A/B: config made byte-identical across trees (corpus md5 identical, .env shared, composer script diff -q IDENTICAL, both resolved agent=z-ai/glm-5.2 code=deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro, both import-clean via polaris_graph.graph smoke test). BASELINE succeeded: cp4_used=agentic, degraded_to_seed=false, real 9-section/2926-word span-grounded report, RACE Overall 0.3782. RENAMED crashed at first outline call on stochastic deepseek truncation before writing any report (its out_dir agentic_report_20260720_014742 is empty) -> no renamed score. First paid renamed attempt (before I fixed the API-key export) exited $0 at the OPENROUTER_API_KEY guard with zero LLM calls, so it did NOT consume the compose budget; the one real renamed compose is the crashed run. No retry per the cap. Honest bottom line: browser exoneration COMPLETE; rename score-exoneration NOT achieved (inconclusive). Artifacts: baseline report /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/outputs/agentic_report_20260720_005837/report.md; RACE result .../third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/polaris_ab_baseline/race_result.txt; logs in scratchpad/{baseline.log,renamed.log,race_baseline.log}; codex gate /tmp/ab_gate.md.
